src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Update OAuth2 resource server JWT configuration to use the new withDefaults() API required by Spring Security 6.1+.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The method jwt() without arguments is deprecated and removed in newer Spring Security versions; calling it without the Customizer argument causes a compilation error.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add static import for Spring Security's withDefaults customizer to support the updated JWT configuration.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The withDefaults() method is used in the updated configuration but was not previously imported, causing a compilation error.

Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 17+ serialization best practices.
Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ module system restrictions on serializable fields.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Compilation error: 'serialVersionUID' is not allowed to be annotated with @Serial unless the class is in the java.base module or the annotation is used correctly; more importantly, in newer Java versions (and strict linters/compilers), annotating serial fields with @Serial is the recommended way to avoid warnings or errors related to serialization metadata access, although strictly speaking, the main driver here is often linter rules or preparation for stricter checks. However, looking at the diff, it's just adding the annotation. Without it, it compiles fine on J17 and J21. But wait, @Serial was introduced in Java 14. If the project is moving to J21, using @Serial is a best practice. Is it breaking? No. The code compiles without it. So it is polishment.

src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml.general_idea: Update web.xml namespace and schema location from Java EE to Jakarta EE to align with Spring Boot 3.x requirements.
src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x runs on Jakarta EE 9+; using the old java.sun.com namespace causes deployment failures or classloading errors because the container expects jakarta.* packages and descriptors.

src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Replace static import of java.lang.String.format with the String.formatted() instance method introduced in Java 15.
src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: While String.format is not removed, the migration intent is to adopt modern Java APIs; however, strictly speaking, this is a polishment as the old code compiles on Java 21. But wait, the prompt says 'bucket=breaking ONLY if the migration won't compile or run... without this change'. String.format still exists. So this is polishment.
